perturbation.original_text: Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States).
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers incorporated in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Canadian advertisers' from entities with 'substantial places of business in Canada' to 'advertisers incorporated in Canada' creates a contradiction. A company can have a substantial place of business without being incorporated in Canada, and vice versa. This discrepancy introduces ambiguity in determining which entity can sell advertising to certain companies, leading to potential disputes. This can contradict with the limitation of advertising on section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers incorporated in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States). All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site. The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.justification: The modification redefines "Canadian advertisers" from having "substantial places of business in Canada" to being "advertisers incorporated in Canada". This creates a contradiction because a company can have a substantial place of business in Canada without being incorporated there, and vice versa. This ambiguity directly impacts which party, MediaLinx or HCI, is entitled to sell advertising to certain companies, directly conflicting with the terms outlined for advertising sales rights, which are at the core of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Applicable remittances and accounting reports will follow under separate cover within sixty (60) days of the end of each quarter. [*] = CERTAIN INFORMATION ON THIS PAGE HAS BEEN OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE COMMISSION.
perturbation.changed_text: Applicable remittances will follow under separate cover within sixty (60) days of the end of each quarter. Accounting records will be sent as soon as possible. [*] = CERTAIN INFORMATION ON THIS PAGE HAS BEEN OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE COMMISSION.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Applicable remittances and accounting reports' to 'Applicable remittances will follow under separate cover within sixty (60) days of the end of each quarter. Accounting records will be sent as soon as possible', creates a contradiction on when should the accounting record be send to the parties. This term change makes the contract very ambiguous because the time it should be send will vary depending on the interpratation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Both Parties shall keep true and accurate books and records of all revenue generated as a result of the Co-Branded Site. Either Party shall have the right from time to time to audit and make extracts of the books and records of the other, insofar as said books or records pertain to the terms of this Agreement. Any such audit shall take place upon not less than seven (7) days advance written notice, during normal business hours of normal business days and at reasonable intervals. Any such audit shall be at the Source: HEALTHCENTRAL COM, S-1/A, 11/8/1999 requesting Party's expense, unless the audit determines that the other party has underpaid by 10% or more. In the event that the calculation of payments herein is determined by a computer-based system, then the "books and records" shall include, without limitation, the machine-sensible data (e.g. punch cards, magnetic tapes, discs; and (d) Both Parties shall provide the other with quarterly accounting reports as necessary. Applicable remittances will follow under separate cover within sixty (60) days of the end of each quarter. Accounting records will be sent as soon as possible. [*] = CERTAIN INFORMATION ON THIS PAGE HAS BEEN OMITTED AND FILED SEPARATELY WITH THE COMMISSION.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that both remittances and accounting reports would be provided within 60 days of the quarter's end. The changed text modifies this, stating that remittances will follow the 60-day timeline, but accounting records will be sent 'as soon as possible.' This creates an ambiguity and contradiction regarding the deadline for accounting reports. The contradicted text, which is the same paragraph containing the modified text, initially implied a consistent 60-day deadline for both, but now specifies two different deadlines. This change introduces uncertainty and potential disputes, directly conflicting with the initial intent of a uniform reporting schedule. The Misaligned Terminology introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All electronic communications to the users of the Co-Branded Site shall be jointly developed, co-branded with each party's respective marks and shall be mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.changed_text: All electronic messages to the users of the Co-Branded Site shall be jointly developed, co-branded with each party's respective marks and shall be mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'communications' to 'messages' creates a contradiction because 'communications' has a broader meaning (it could mean calls, messages, emails), while 'messages' are just textual interaction between two parties. This contradiction makes the term on section 10 ambiguos, because it is not clear that only electronic textual interactions should be joint developed, co-branded with each party's respective marks and shall be mutually agreed upon. This could create legal gaps and legal voids.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the first six (6) months from the Launch date, the primary branding for the Co-Branded Site shall remain HealthyWay. In addition to the foregoing and subject to mutual design approval, a "Powered By HealthCentral" or such other appropriate tag line shall appear on the Co-Branded Site and shall be approximately 80% of the font size of the HealthyWay title and will be close to the HealthyWay brand. Thereafter, the HealthyWay and Health Central trademarks shall be co-branded in a mutually agreed upon method. The URL for the Co-Branded Site shall remain healthyway.sympatico.ca. A separate redirect URL may be set up for the Co-Branded Site, e.g. healthcentral.sympatico.ca. All electronic messages to the users of the Co-Branded Site shall be jointly developed, co-branded with each party's respective marks and shall be mutually agreed upon.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'communications' to 'messages' narrows the scope of what requires joint development, co-branding, and mutual agreement. The original text's broader term 'communications' encompassed various forms of electronic interaction, while 'messages' limits it to textual interactions. This creates ambiguity in the contradicted section. It is unclear whether emails or calls would be subject to the same restrictions, thus leading to a contradiction that could cause legal gaps and legal voids.
